goal: Diagnose and mitigate system freezes that appear when the robot is shaken strongly, with evidence pointing to the MPU6050 path.
deliverable: A diagnosis-backed firmware hardening change set that improves runtime recovery from IMU/I2C transport faults caused or exposed by motion.
constraints: Preserve existing control and host-link behavior.
Prefer bounded recovery over large architectural changes.
Be explicit that connector or wiring intermittency can still exceed what software can mask.
acceptance_criteria: The firmware should no longer rely on a permanently healthy I2C1 bus during motion.
MPU6050 and SSD1306 runtime accesses should attempt bounded bus recovery after I2C faults.
The change should be traceable to code paths that can actually freeze the main loop.
product_acceptance_criteria: Large motion should be less likely to hard-freeze the board.
If IMU data becomes invalid due to motion or intermittent contact, the system should degrade and recover rather than hang.
manual_spot_checks: Shake the robot strongly after boot and observe whether host stream and control loop continue updating.
Check whether the board avoids long apparent freezes or watchdog-triggered resets.
Check whether IMU validity may drop temporarily without causing system lockup.
completion_language_policy: Do not claim the motion-induced freeze is fully eliminated without hardware-in-loop validation.
delivery_truth_contract: Only claim code-level recovery improvements and static evidence from the firmware paths.
non_goals: Claiming the plug-in MPU6050 hardware is fault-free.
Rewriting all I2C interactions to DMA or interrupt mode.
Changing the IMU fusion algorithm based on this symptom alone.
inferred_assumptions: Violent motion itself does not make Attitude_Update loop forever; the more plausible freeze path is transport-layer I2C failure or bus lock.
A shared I2C1 fault can affect both MPU6050 and SSD1306, amplifying the visible freeze symptoms.
A bounded bus recovery plus retry strategy is an appropriate next step before deeper hardware redesign.
